LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS-DEFENDANT ARTSION KHATKEVICH'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. THERE BEING NO APPEARANCE OR OBJECTION, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EXPRESS INDEMNITY AND OVERRULED AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. B SIDE DOES NOT DISPUTE MR. KHATKEVICH'S STATEMENT THAT B SIDE IS WITHDRAWING ITS SECTION 7160 CAUSE OF ACTION. NONE OF THE OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION ARE UNCERTAIN. THE FRAUD, SECTION 7028.4(B), SECTION 7031(B) AND BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY ALLEGED. B SIDE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES ALL THE ELEMENTS TO STATE EACH OF THOSE CLAIMS, INCLUDING AN ACTIONABLE MISREPRESENTATION IN THE FORM OF AN ADVERTISEMENT, FRAUDULENT INTENT, A CONTINUING VIOLATION FOR PURPOSES OF SECTION 7028.4, AND IDENTIFYING THE TERMS IN THE CONCORD PROJECT THAT WERE BREACHED. THE EXPRESS INDEMNITY CLAIM FAILS BECAUSE THE INDEMNITY LANGUAGE RELIED ON IS NOT REASONABLY SUSCEPTIBLE TO B SIDE'S PROFFERED INTERPRETATION THAT THE LANGUAGE COVERS FIRST PARTY CONDUCT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: LAURA A. REDING, CSR 	711, EMAIL: LREDING@SFTC.ORG.

Plaintiff/Cross-Defendant Khatkevich'S Notice And Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Answer
Off Calendar May-10-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF/CORSS-DEFENDANT ARTSIOM KHATKEVICH'S Motion For Order Granting Leave To Amend Answer.

Cross-defendant Artsiom Khatkevich's motion for leave to amend answer is granted in substantial part. Mr. Khatsiom is granted leave to amend his answer by including all of his proposed new affirmative defenses except proposed affirmative defense 17 labeled "illegal contract." Proposed affirmative defense 17 appears to be based solely on opposing party's discovery responses that the contract is illegal as a result of Mr. Khatsiom's alleged failure to comply with the contractor licensing requirements. That is not a permissible basis for Mr. Khatsiom to allege an affirmative defense of illegal contract.

Counsel for B Side is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-01-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Cross-Defendant Artsiom Khatkevich'S Demurrer To Second Amended Cross-Complaint (Per Order Filed On Sep-28-2017)
Off Calendar Oct-12-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance.

RULING: There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS-DEFENDANT ARTSION KHATKEVICH's Demurrer To Second Amended Cross-Complaint as follows: Cross-defendant Artsiom Khatkevich's demurrer to all causes of action alleged in the second amended cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant B Side, Inc. is sustained without leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for express indemnity and overruled as to all other causes of action. B Side does not dispute Mr. Khatkevich's statement that B Side is withdrawing its section 7160 cause of action. None of the other causes of action are uncertain. The fraud, section 7028.4(b), section 7031(b) and breach of contract causes of action are adequately alleged. B Side sufficiently alleges all the elements to state each of those claims, including an actionable misrepresentation in the form of an advertisement, fraudulent intent, a continuing violation for purposes of section 7028.4, and identifying the terms in the Concord project that were breached. The express indemnity claim fails because the indemnity language relied on is not reasonably susceptible to B Side's proffered interpretation that the language covers first party conduct.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Laura A. Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org.

[After hearing, the Court signed the proposed order submitted by email.]

C.C.P. 1005(b). The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.
